trivia_bullets.text: Wishcoin Crab was first printed in Guilds of Ravnica (October 2018) as a blue common — a 2/5 defensive creature in a set focused on Ravnica's guild factions. The Guilds of Ravnica printing carries identical flavor text and the same James Paick art. Its M21 reprint recycles the card into a fresh Standard environment. [Scryfall: GRN/60]

Flavor text: "What wishes do they grant? Mostly pinching-related ones." —Omik, superintendent of waterworks [Scryfall]. Omik is a throwaway NPC voice — a Ravnica bureaucrat whose dry, sardonic comment grounds the magical creature in the mundane infrastructure of the city-plane. The joke deflates the 'wishcoin' mystique: these crabs were presumably tossed into fountains or waterways for luck and have developed a reputation.

Oracle text: empty (no abilities). Wishcoin Crab is a vanilla 2/5 for 4 mana — no rules text at all. Its statistical identity is purely as a defensive blue blocker with an unusually high toughness-to-cost ratio for a common. EDHREC rank 19,189 reflects minimal Commander adoption; vanilla creatures rarely find Commander homes. Legal in Pauper, where the 2/5 body at common sees occasional mention as a budget wall. [Scryfall]

Art by James Paick, who has illustrated across multiple MTG sets. The coastal waterfall scene — blue-purple crustacean, gold-spotted shell, warm dawn light — is atypical for Ravnica, which usually features urban environments. The Ravnica printing's outdoor setting implies the waterworks infrastructure extends to natural waterways on the city-plane's outer rings. [Scryfall]
